roadmap.milestone_count_rationale: 5 milestones: beginner with a wide gap from foundational concepts to Senior Backend Engineer, requiring multiple identity transformations and a structured progression through core engineering, scalable systems, and architectural design.

vision_profile.current_state: An aspiring professional with foundational technical skills, eager to transition into a specialized backend engineering role.
vision_profile.main_blocker: Lack of specialized backend system design knowledge, practical experience with scalable architectures, and understanding of senior-level responsibilities.
vision_profile.top_motivation: To build robust, scalable, and high-performance backend systems and lead technical initiatives in a senior capacity.

milestones.label: M01
milestones.o: The learner can design and implement basic data structures, write clean, efficient Python code, and understand core programming paradigms essential for backend development.
milestones.identity_statement: You are building the bedrock of your engineering career, mastering the fundamental principles that underpin all robust backend systems.

project.title: Basic API Service with Data Persistence
project.description: Build a simple RESTful API service using Python, capable of performing CRUD operations on a local data store (e.g., SQLite). The service should handle basic requests and responses, demonstrating clean code and data management.
project.deliverable: A deployed Python API service with a clear README, API documentation, and unit tests, accessible via a local endpoint.
seeded_errors: The API incorrectly handles concurrent requests, leading to data corruption in the local database. The learner must identify and fix the race condition.

scenario.title: Debugging a Python Script for Data Processing
Optimizing Data Retrieval Performance
Resolving Git Merge Conflicts

science.desc: A critical bug is reported in a legacy Python script. You need to quickly identify the root cause, which involves understanding unfamiliar code and data flow, and propose a fix under pressure.

scenario.title: Refactoring a Monolithic Class
Diagnosing Network Connectivity Issues
Designing an API for a New Feature

science.desc: Describe the core principles of OOP and how they contribute to building maintainable and extensible backend systems. Provide a real-world example.

milestones.label: M02
milestones.o: The learner can build robust backend services with persistent storage, design effective APIs, and implement secure data handling practices for web applications.
milestones.identity_statement: You are now a capable backend engineer, building the essential services and data layers that power modern applications.

project.title: E-commerce Product Catalog Service
project.description: Develop a backend service for an e-commerce product catalog. This service should manage products, categories, and inventory, exposing a RESTful API for client applications. It must use a relational database for data persistence.
project.deliverable: A deployed backend service with a PostgreSQL database, comprehensive API documentation (e.g., OpenAPI/Swagger), unit and integration tests, and a basic client (e.g., Postman collection) demonstrating functionality.
seeded_errors: The product search endpoint returns inconsistent results due to improper indexing or a flawed query logic. The learner must identify the performance bottleneck and data inconsistency.

scenario.title: Optimizing a Slow Database Query
Debugging ORM Query Performance
Choosing the Right Database for a New Feature

science.desc: A new feature requires storing user preferences with high read/write throughput and flexible schema. You need to decide between a relational and a NoSQL database, justifying your choice based on technical requirements and future scalability.

scenario.title: Refining an API for Better Usability
Implementing Secure User Authentication
Integrating a New Feature into an Existing API

science.desc: You need to design a new API for a user authentication service. What are the key considerations for security, scalability, and developer experience? How would you handle authentication and authorization?

milestones.label: M03
milestones.o: The learner can design and implement distributed systems, microservices, and optimize for scalability and performance, handling high traffic and complex data flows.
milestones.identity_statement: You are now building systems that can grow and adapt, mastering the art of distributed computing and high-performance backend architectures.

project.title: Distributed Task Queue & Worker System
project.description: Design and implement a distributed task queue system using a message broker (e.g., RabbitMQ or Kafka) and multiple worker services. The system should process asynchronous tasks (e.g., image processing, email sending) reliably and scalably.
project.deliverable: A deployed system with a message broker, at least two distinct worker services, a client to submit tasks, and monitoring dashboards showing task processing rates and worker health.
seeded_errors: Tasks are occasionally processed multiple times due to worker crashes or message broker misconfiguration. The learner must implement idempotency and ensure exactly-once processing semantics.

scenario.title: Handling Network Partitions in a Distributed Database
Decomposing a Monolith into Microservices
Ensuring Message Delivery in a High-Throughput System

science.desc: A critical microservice is experiencing intermittent failures under heavy load, leading to data inconsistencies across dependent services. You need to diagnose the root cause, which could be a race condition, deadlocks, or improper error handling, and propose a resilient solution.

scenario.title: Scaling a Database for 10x Traffic
Reducing Latency with a Caching Layer
Debugging a Concurrent Data Processing Pipeline

science.desc: You are tasked with scaling a high-traffic API that frequently accesses a database. Describe how you would approach identifying bottlenecks and implementing solutions like caching, database sharding, and asynchronous processing to handle increased load.

milestones.label: M04
milestones.o: The learner can build highly available, secure, and observable backend systems, implementing robust testing, monitoring, and deployment strategies.
milestones.identity_statement: You are now building systems that stand the test of time and failure, ensuring continuous operation and data integrity in complex environments.

project.title: Secure & Observable User Management Service
project.description: Develop a user management microservice with robust authentication, authorization, and data encryption. Implement comprehensive logging, monitoring, and alerting to ensure high availability and quick issue detection. Integrate with an external identity provider (mocked or real).
project.deliverable: A deployed microservice with secure API endpoints, user registration/login, role-based access control, detailed logs (e.g., ELK stack), metrics (e.g., Prometheus/Grafana), and automated tests covering security and functionality.
seeded_errors: A security vulnerability is discovered where an authenticated user can access data belonging to other users by manipulating API request parameters. The learner must identify and patch the authorization bypass.

scenario.title: Responding to a SQL Injection Attempt
Diagnosing a Production Outage with Logs and Metrics
Identifying Latency Spikes in a Multi-Service Request Flow

science.desc: Your production system is experiencing a sudden increase in error rates and latency, but no specific service is showing obvious signs of failure. You need to use monitoring and tracing tools to pinpoint the exact bottleneck or failing component in a complex distributed environment.

scenario.title: Debugging a Failing Integration Test
Troubleshooting a Failed Deployment Pipeline
Debugging a Failing Kubernetes Deployment

science.desc: Explain the importance of CI/CD in modern backend development. How would you set up a CI/CD pipeline for a microservice, and what steps would you include to ensure reliability and quick recovery from deployment failures?

milestones.label: M05
milestones.o: The learner can architect complex, large-scale backend systems, make critical design trade-offs, and lead technical initiatives, demonstrating expertise in advanced system design and strategic thinking.
milestones.identity_statement: You are now a leader in backend engineering, shaping the future of systems and guiding teams to build innovative and robust solutions.

project.title: Real-time Analytics & Recommendation Engine Backend
project.description: Design and implement the backend for a real-time analytics and recommendation engine. This involves ingesting high-volume data streams, processing them in near real-time, storing aggregated data, and serving personalized recommendations via an API. Consider data consistency, latency, and fault tolerance.
project.deliverable: A comprehensive system design document, a deployed prototype demonstrating data ingestion, real-time processing (e.g., with Kafka Streams or Flink), data storage (e.g., Cassandra/ClickHouse), and a recommendation API. Include performance benchmarks and a disaster recovery plan.
seeded_errors: The real-time recommendation API occasionally serves stale recommendations due to replication lag or incorrect cache invalidation. The learner must diagnose the data consistency issue and implement a solution for eventual consistency with acceptable latency.

scenario.title: Designing a Highly Consistent Distributed Payment System
Designing a Real-time Fraud Detection System
Migrating a Monolithic Application to Cloud-Native Architecture

science.desc: A new product requires a global, highly available, and low-latency data storage solution for user profiles. You need to design the data architecture, considering consistency models, replication strategies, and cloud-native services to meet these demanding requirements.

scenario.title: Justifying a Major Technology Stack Change to Stakeholders
Mediating a Technical Dispute Between Team Members

skills.title: AI-Powered Backend Systems
lessons: Designing for AI Inference at Scale

scenario.title: Designing an AI-Powered Content Moderation System

science.desc: You are leading the design of a new critical backend system. How do you balance innovation with stability, and what processes would you put in place to ensure the architectural decisions align with long-term business goals and technical feasibility?

laugh_test.reason: Laugh test passed — filler ratio 10% (3/30). Full JD validation pending.

no_spectators.reason: No spectator violations — every skill has applied activities

dag_clean.reason: DAG clean — 30 unique skills, all prerequisites backward-only

ai_first.reason: AI-first check passed — all milestones have multi-layer AI work
